name: Rudy Williams
abstract: Rudolph Williams (September 21, 1909 – August 30, 1954) was an American jazz alto saxophonist. He was born in Newark, New Jersey, United States.

concerts.label: Horizons in Jazz featuring Dizzy Gillespie, George Shearing, and Others

about_quotes.speaker: Howard McGhee
about_quotes.evidence: First we had Budd Johnson on tenor, and then we switched over and got Rudy Williams. It was a good band.

about_quotes.speaker: Howard McGhee

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Oscar Pettiford was the leader of this group, and it included Rudy Williams on saxophone
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Lamb
about_quotes.evidence: But up there in Montana I got the chance to work with some of the musicians there and there was one fellow by the name of Rudy Williams
about_quotes.doc_title: John Lamb interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: James Moody
about_quotes.evidence: But there was a guy by the name of Rudy Williams (phonetic sp.) who played with the Savoy Salton (phonetic sp.) and he was from Newark.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Moody — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
